title: The Feature Extraction Pipeline in Computer Vision
description: A flowchart illustrating the typical stages and methods involved in extracting meaningful features from a raw input image for computer vision tasks.
relationships: The Raw Input Image is processed by Pre-processing to normalize it, which is then analyzed by Feature Detection to find key points, which are then passed to Feature Description to create a numerical representation, resulting in a Feature Vector that is fed into a Machine Learning Model for classification or other tasks.
